column2: # Manually compile the effect sizes from Analysis 1 (both visits) and Analysis 2 (both visits)
analysis = c(rep("Global mixed ANOVA\n(both visits, N=19)", 3),
significant = c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)
p_forest <- ggplot(effects_df, aes(x = pes, y = label, color = significant)) +
annotate("rect", xmin = 0,    xmax = 0.01, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
annotate("rect", xmin = 0.01, xmax = 0.06, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
annotate("rect", xmin = 0.06, xmax = 0.14, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
annotate("rect", xmin = 0.14, xmax = Inf,  ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
annotate("text", x = 0.005, y = Inf, label = "negl.",  vjust = 1.5, size = 2.9, colour = "grey40") +
annotate("text", x = 0.035, y = Inf, label = "small",  vjust = 1.5, size = 2.9, colour = "grey40") +
annotate("text", x = 0.10,  y = Inf, label = "medium", vjust = 1.5, size = 2.9, colour = "grey40") +
annotate("text", x = 0.30,  y = Inf, label = "large",  vjust = 1.5, size = 2.9, colour = "grey40") +
geom_segment(aes(x = 0, xend = pes, yend = label), linewidth = 0.5) +
subtitle = "Only the effect of FAC group reaches a large effect size and statistical significance.",
"Note: non-significant effects with large \u03B7\u00B2p (e.g., Sensor in FAC 1-3) suggest possible effects\n",
"masked by low statistical power (n=3). Interpret with caution."
